data.body:
PASS 1 review (T8 — LLM/prompt structural-completeness overlay): CLEAN, no findings.

Tool-grounded verification of every referenced symbol/enum the plan names:
- Verdict vocabulary `certified|mismatch|foreign_key|unsigned` is fully enumerated in the plan AND backed by src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json (enum) — no undefined-but-referenced enum.
- Signing surface: sign_manifest, verify_signature, verify_record, signing_key, key_fingerprint, compute_signature, parse_manifest, head_sha — all present in src/rebar/signing.py.
- LLM runtime: Runner protocol (runner.py:71), RunRequest (runner.py:29), get_runner (runner.py:236), run_workflow (workflow/executor.py:584), RunResult (workflow/executor.py:457), validate_structured (findings.py:247), verify_completion (completion.py:169) — all present.
- Prompt/contract: get_prompt (prompts.py:173), resolve_prompt (prompts.py:648) — present.

T8 probes that are N/A by construction: this plan DOCUMENTS existing machinery rather than authoring a new LLM decision protocol, so there is no new counter/state increment, no new fallback sub-step, and no sub-agent orchestration whose locality could regress. AC7 + the referenced anti-drift gate (tests/unit/test_reuse_surface_doc.py, which introspects each documented callable) directly satisfy the structural-completeness concern by binding doc signatures to source.

No structural gaps proven. PASS for T8.
